titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Serge Vandercam Papers
titlestmt.author: by Lucretia Baskin

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday November 27, 2024 at 23:22

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains correspondence, writings, photographs, artwork, printed material, and other papers by or relating to Belgian photographer and artist Serge Vandercam.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 15 (phonograph record): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Philippe Luigi on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2018, and the Elizabeth W. Manwaring Fund, 2019.

bioghist.head: Serge Vandercam (1924-2005)
bioghist.p: Serge Vandercam was a Belgian photographer and artist associated with the European avant-garde art movement known as CoBrA. Vandercam was born on March 31, 1924 in Copenhagen, Denmark, to a Belgian father and an Italian mother. He learned photography at the Tallon photogravure studio in Brussels and worked as a street photographer in Antwerp after World War II, briefly studying at the École Belge de Photographie et de Cinématographie in Brussels. After the dissolution of CoBrA in 1951, he branched out to other art forms, beginning with painting in 1952, filmmaking in 1957, ceramics in 1960, and sculpture in 1972. In the late 1950s, Vandercam also was involved with a short-lived Belgian periodical titled Plus . Vandercam taught at École Nationale des Arts Visuels de La Cambre in Brussels from 1979 to 1989 and served as director of the École des Beaux-Arts in Wavre, Belgium, from 1981 to 1989. In 1993, he was elected to the Royal Academy of Science, Letters and Fine Arts of Belgium and in 1998 was appointed chairman of the organization.
Vandercam was married to Talia Vandercam, with whom he had one son, Joel. He died on March 10, 2005 in Brussels.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Serge Vandercam Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, writings, photographs, artwork, printed material, and other papers by or relating to Belgian photographer and artist Serge Vandercam. Materials document Vandercam's association with the avant-garde CoBrA movement, his work at the modern art periodical Plus , exhibitions, and other professional endeavors. Correspondents include Pierre Alechinsky, Karel Appel, Enrico Baj, Gianni Bertini, Marcel Broodthaers, Constant, Corneille, Christian Dotremont, Edward Jaguer, Joseph Noiret, Gabriel Piqueray, Marcel Piqueray, and Yasse Tabuchi. Artwork consists of sketchbooks and a few watercolors and pencil drawings by Vandercam. Photographs and negatives depict exhibitions, among them the last CoBrA show in 1951; sculpture, ceramics, and paintings by Vandercam and other artists; friends; and family. The collection also contains ephemera and exhibition catalogs and one 45 rpm sound recording.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Serge Vandercam Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Writings and Other Papers, 1946-2001, undated. II. Photographs, 1947-2003, undated. III. Printed Material, 1948-1970, undated. IV. Sound Recording, 1969.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence; writings; files related to three issues of Plus ; sketchbooks, drawings, and paintings by Serge Vandercam; and materials related to film and theater productions. Included are drafts, paste-ups, proofs, and artwork for books illustrated by Vandercam and Marcel et Gabriel Piqueray's Non Inhibited Poems , and manuscripts by Christian Dotremont and Joseph Noiret. Correspondents include artists and writers associated with CoBrA, such as Pierre Alechinsky, Karel Appel, Enrico Baj, Constant Nieuwenhuys, Corneille (Guillaume Cornelis van Beverloo), Dotremont, Edward Jaguer, Noiret, and Yasse Tabuchi, as well as Gianni Bertini, Marcel Broodthaers, the Piqueray brothers, and various galleries. The series also contains several identity cards and certificates belonging to Vandercam.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two linocut proofs by Hace Vria.

scopecontent.p: Large artwork proofs.

scopecontent.p: Notes, page proofs, printed version.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proofs, 9 intaglio prints marked "E.A.," 2 prospectuses.

did.unittitle: Brussels Art Quintet, Vas-y-Voir , album cover design by Vandercam

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph for cover, album cover for 45 rpm recording of poems by Marcel and Gabriel Piqueray.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poster for Ici Paris with autograph manuscript poem by Joseph Noiret on verso.

did.unittitle: Les ports belges : Henri Storck et Jean Raine

title.part: Questions après la chute d'Icare sous le regard des oiseaux perplexes

did.unittitle: Plan du Théatre royal de Bruxelles
Carnets à dessins de Serge Vandercam
Cahier d'ecolier a moitie rempli contenant des textes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "1 aquarelle signee Vandercam, 1 dessin aux crayons de couleurs signe et date Vandercam, 1 encre de Chine signee et datee 56, 1 bloc-note comprenant 4 dessins origineaux de SV, 4 dessins origineaux de SV non-signee."

did.unittitle: Essai de signature a l'encre lithographique (in folio) au recto et au verso de Dypreau

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 unique artists' books.

title.part: Non inhibited poems 1949-1954. Interventions de Pierre-Paul Rubens et Serge Vandercam

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Belgian identity card, membership card for professional organization, and certificate for Vandercam's nomination to the Ordre de Leopold.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes gold medal certificate from Accademia Italia delle Arti e del Lavoro.

did.unittitle: "Les Chiens" by Tone Brulin, draft, typescript, corrected
Cards from Pierre Aleschinsky, Gianni Bertini

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographs by and of Serge Vandercam, his artwork, colleagues such as Pierre Aleschinsky and Joseph Noiret, and CoBrA exhibitions. Also included are a number of negatives and contact prints.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of the last CoBrA exhibition in Liège, Belgium before the group disbanded, friends and family, and travels by Vandercam.

did.unittitle: Photographies d'oeuvres envoyées par les artistes
Dossier de photographies prises par SV au Musées du Cinquantenaire

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 9 photographs by Serge Vanderkamp. With photographs (including negatives) by Raymond Konig of Vandercamp taking the pictures.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of ephemera and exhibition catalogs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Catalogues d'expositions personelles ou collectives de Serge Vandercam

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two holiday cards with printed and watercolor decoration by Vandercam, and printed exhibition announcement with crayon drawing by Serge Vandercam.

did.unittitle: Catalogues d'expositions personelles ou collectives de Serge Vandercam
Catalogues d'exposition d'artistes proches de Serge Vandercam

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes invitation with crayon drawing and inscription by Serge Vandercam.

did.unittitle: Catalogues d'exposition d'artistes proches de Serge Vandercam

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of one 45 rpm record by the Brussels Art Quintet for which Serge Vandercam provided the album cover art.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
